_meta: MP-04 visual review rubric. Criteria are data, not code, so an operator can tune what a review looks for without touching the actuator. `weight` orders the summary; `severity` decides whether a finding blocks delivery. Anti-pattern checks are weighted above craft on purpose: a deck that is merely tidy but looks machine-made is the failure mode this loop exists to catch.

criteria.title: Text fits its frame
criteria.prompt: Does any text run past its box, clip at an edge, or collide with another element? Name the slide and the element.

criteria.title: Alignment and margins
criteria.prompt: Are elements aligned to a consistent grid, with even margins? Flag anything visibly off-axis or with uneven gutters.

criteria.title: Legibility and contrast
criteria.prompt: Is every text legible against its background at a glance? Flag low-contrast pairings and text that is too small for the medium.

criteria.title: Visual hierarchy
criteria.prompt: Does the eye land on the most important thing first? Flag slides where everything is the same weight or the emphasis is on the wrong element.

criteria.title: Machine-made tells
criteria.prompt: Does this look generated? Flag decorative accent lines under titles, gratuitous color bars, uniform font weight throughout, everything centered, and stock gradient backgrounds. These are the specific tells that mark a deck as AI-made.

criteria.title: Density and whitespace
criteria.prompt: Is any slide overloaded, or so empty it looks unfinished? Suggest splitting or merging.

criteria.title: Cross-slide consistency
criteria.prompt: Do repeated elements (headers, footers, logos, body position) sit in the same place on every slide? Flag drift.

iteration._meta: Anthropic's generator/evaluator loop runs 5-15 rounds; this starts at 3 because each round costs a render plus a model call, and the deterministic layout fit already removed the mechanical failures a long loop would otherwise chase.
